name: Embalse del Burguillo — gravel circular by the río Alberche
name_original: Embalse del Burguillo — gravel circular por el río Alberche
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Embalse de El Burguillo traverse a diverse landscape at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos, characterized by the reservoir itself and a Mediterranean ecosystem. The region features the Alberche River, which feeds the reservoir, and is bordered by the Valle de Iruelas Natural Park, known for its chestnut forests. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, from routes along the reservoir's edge to paths with significant elevation changes in the surrounding hills. Best touring cycling routes around Embalse de El Burguillo The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse del Burguillo — gravel circular by the río Alberche, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the Alberche River on gravel surfaces. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Burguillo Reservoir – Burguillo Reservoir loop from Recepción, a difficult 27.8 miles (44.7 km) path. This route offers extensive views of the reservoir as it loops around its perimeter. Local touring cyclists also love the Navaluenga Valley – Puente Viejo de Navaluenga loop from Albergue Sierra de Gredos, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through the Navaluenga Valley, often completed in about 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Embalse de El Burguillo is defined by reservoir views, routes along the Alberche River, and paths through the Sierra de Gredos foothills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy valley rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Embalse de El Burguillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from 10 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de El Burguillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Embalse de El Burguillo?
A: The touring cycling routes around Embalse de El Burguillo offer a diverse landscape. You'll find paths along the reservoir's edge, routes following the Alberche River, and trails through the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos. The terrain varies from gravel surfaces, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105175902/embalse-del-burguillo-gravel-circular-by-the-rio-alberche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Burguillo — gravel circular by the río Alberche&lt;/a&gt;, to more paved or compacted surfaces, with significant elevation changes in the surrounding hills.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for various ability levels. While many routes are challenging, you can find easier options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33803504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga Valley – Puente Viejo de Navaluenga loop from Albergue Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.4-mile (10.4 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete, leading through the scenic Navaluenga Valley.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Embalse de El Burguillo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself, the oldest and largest in Ávila province, and enjoy views of the Alberche River. The area borders the Valle de Iruelas Natural Park, known for its magnificent chestnut forests and as a special bird protection area for black vultures. Keep an eye out for viewpoints like 'El Banco del Burguillo' for spectacular panoramas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural landscapes, you can explore historic towns such as El Barraco and El Tiemblo. These towns offer cultural excursions and access to the ancient Celtic sculptures known as the Bulls of Guisando, providing a glimpse into the region's rich history.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling around Embalse de El Burguillo?
A: The Mediterranean ecosystem of Embalse de El Burguillo makes it enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. While winter can bring snowy mountain peaks, adding to the scenery, it might also mean colder conditions. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable during that season.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir – Burguillo Reservoir loop from Recepción&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 27.8-mile (44.7 km) path that offers extensive views as it circles the reservoir.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Embalse de El Burguillo?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes available around Embalse de El Burguillo on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 4 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 52 difficult routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Embalse de El Burguillo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from 10 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning reservoir views, and the routes that follow the Alberche River and wind through the Sierra de Gredos foothills.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are numerous difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31447553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga Valley – El Berraco loop from Río Alberche&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.7 km route with over 750 meters of ascent, providing a demanding ride through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Alberche River?
A: Yes, the Alberche River feeds the Embalse de El Burguillo, and several touring cycling routes follow its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105175902/embalse-del-burguillo-gravel-circular-by-the-rio-alberche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Burguillo — gravel circular by the río Alberche&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering beautiful views along the river on a gravel surface.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for a balance between easy and difficult, there are moderate options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33803852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Las Cruceras&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 23.3 km route with over 400 meters of elevation gain, offering a good workout without being overly strenuous.
tips: Beautiful views that I find when I reach this point. A natural viewpoint of the Alberche river that is undoubtedly worth visiting.

The Burguillo reservoir is the first reservoir of the Alberche river, it was inaugurated in 1913 and it is the oldest dam in Spain. The route follows the GR-10 long-distance trail along the shore of the artificial lake, one section is a fantastic single track without major technical complications but has several rocky steps.
Located at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos, the Burguillo reservoir is an ideal area to practice all kinds of activities. It offers exceptional views from several points.
